characters.description: A 21-year-old mortal and long-term cashier at Chirot's Hypermarket. Poor and overworked, he relies on a shortcut through the Twenty-Fifth District to keep his commute manageable and discovers an open passage after being forced to take another route.
characters.name: Freddy Stern

characters.description: Freddy's older neighbor and an established acquaintance in their shared building.
characters.name: Sharon

characters.description: The habitually late night-shift cashier who relieves Freddy at Chirot's Hypermarket.
characters.name: Jenny

characters.description: The power who effectively controls the Twenty-Fifth District and holds contractual rights connected to the recent passage incident. After obtaining Freddy's report, the lord takes a personal interest in finding the manifestation linked to the attack.
characters.name: City lord

characters.description: A rare skeletal remnant found near Freddy in the nether echo. It names itself Bloodshed, addresses Freddy as its master, obeys direct instructions, and says its nature prevents it from deliberately killing him.
characters.name: Bloodshed

characters.description: A hostile patriarch who claims the dangerous remnant connected to the passage incident. The city lord rejects his attempt to treat Freddy's property as his own, prompting the patriarch to direct his anger toward Freddy.
characters.name: Craven patriarch

characters.description: A 21-year-old former Grade 7 student at Aroon Arch Academy. He has stepped away from its ten-year program and regards his early departure as a failure, although his family supports his decision to take time away.
characters.name: Mark Affronte

characters.description: A nationally prominent talk-show host and powerful lord who approaches Freddy after his passage discovery. She offers to make his experience the subject of a public interview and brings him into a carefully managed professional arrangement.
characters.name: Madame Moorlap

characters.description: Madame Moorlap's assistant and Freddy's practical liaison. He supervises Freddy's relocation to a furnished apartment, provides access to a large daily budget, and coordinates the preparations surrounding the planned interview.
characters.name: Matt Canstone

characters.description: Freddy's assigned medical examiner after his new training arrangement begins. He confirms that Freddy has only water affinity, sets strict health precautions, and privately recognizes serious muscular and cardiac problems that he does not disclose to Freddy.
characters.name: Clinic doctor

characters.description: Mark's younger sister and a newly admitted academy student. The high cost of treating Mark's worsening injury places the family's ability to fund her education at risk, making her future part of the pressure surrounding him.
characters.name: Sarah

characters.description: A business proposer who addresses the Craven council as it decides what to do with hundreds of political prisoners. He offers a labor-based plan that would make the captives economically useful instead of having them immediately executed.
characters.name: Stephen White

characters.description: A covert expedition staffer posing as a debtor. After seeing Freddy compete in the Camp Violet arena, he begins examining Freddy's background and wrongly infers that an influential political sponsor is protecting him.
characters.name: Undercover expedition employee

characters.description: A silver-haired assistant in Camp Violet's foraging program. This staff member watches the workers on behalf of the expedition and possesses a poison-focused talent, giving the role greater significance than ordinary instructional work suggests.
characters.name: Silver-haired foraging assistant

characters.description: The silver-haired foraging assistant, now identified as Peter Vane. He is a disguised two-star Camp Violet staff member who offers Freddy help escaping in exchange for future support from the powerful master he mistakenly believes Freddy serves.
characters.name: Peter Vane

characters.description: An exceptionally large and durable Camp Violet fighter matched against Freddy in a sanctioned arena bout. His intimidating size makes him a formidable test of the combat ability Freddy has developed in secret.
characters.name: Skull Crusher

characters.description: An Earth-affinity combatant appointed to lead the three-person Camp Violet squad sent into the caves to seize Freddy. She directs the pursuit after the administrators decide that he should be captured rather than killed immediately.
characters.name: Capture-squad leader

characters.description: A fire-affinity member of the three-person squad hunting Freddy in the caves. His ability to sense heat contributes to the search as the team follows signs of a hidden route beyond Camp Violet's familiar territory.
characters.name: Capture-squad fire combatant

characters.description: A tall Earth-affinity fighter who joins the Camp Violet capture squad pursuing Freddy. She enters the caves with the squad leader and a heat-sensing fire combatant as their search reaches a concealed passage.
characters.name: Capture-squad Earth combatant
